name: Stable Diffusion 1.5 inference
description: Generate images with the classic SD 1.5 model. Runs on modest GPUs; 4GB VRAM minimum, 8GB recommended for comfortable batch generation.

name: SDXL inference
description: Generate high-resolution images with Stable Diffusion XL. Requires at least 12GB VRAM; 24GB recommended for full-quality 1024×1024 outputs without offloading.

name: Flux inference
description: Run Black Forest Labs Flux.1 models for state-of-the-art text-to-image generation. The full model requires 24GB; quantised versions can fit in 16GB.

name: LLM inference 7B
description: Serve 7-billion-parameter language models such as Mistral 7B or Llama-3 8B. FP16 weights need ~14GB; 4-bit quantisation brings this under 8GB.

name: LLM inference 13B
description: Serve 13-billion-parameter models such as Llama-2 13B or CodeLlama 13B. FP16 requires ~26GB; a single RTX 4090 works with 4-bit quantisation.

name: LLM inference 34B
description: Serve 34-billion-parameter models such as CodeLlama 34B or Yi-34B. FP16 needs ~68GB; a single A100 80GB handles it, or two A100 40GB cards with tensor parallelism.

name: LLM inference 70B
description: Serve Llama-3 70B or similar models. FP16 requires ~140GB; two H100 80GB or four A100 40GB cards are typical production setups.

name: LLM inference 405B
description: Serve Llama-3.1 405B or similar frontier-scale models. FP16 requires ~810GB; typically needs 8×H100 SXM or FP8 quantisation on fewer GPUs.

name: LoRA training
description: Train low-rank adapters (LoRA/QLoRA) on top of a base LLM or diffusion model. Much lighter than full fine-tuning; a single RTX 4090 handles most 7B LoRA jobs.

name: Full fine-tuning (7B)
description: Full-parameter fine-tuning of a 7B model with AdamW optimizer states. Requires ~4× model size in VRAM; a single A100 80GB is the practical minimum for a comfortable batch size.

name: Full fine-tuning (70B)
description: Full-parameter fine-tuning of a 70B model. Requires hundreds of GB for weights, gradients, and optimizer states; typically uses 8×H100 SXM with FSDP or DeepSpeed ZeRO-3.

name: Whisper transcription
description: Transcribe audio with OpenAI Whisper (large-v3 or distil-Whisper). Whisper Large needs ~10GB; smaller variants or batched processing fit in 2–8GB.

name: Embedding generation
description: Generate text embeddings with models like BGE, E5, or OpenAI-compatible servers. Very low VRAM; throughput scales almost linearly with batch size.

name: Video generation
description: Generate short video clips with models such as CogVideoX, Wan, or HunyuanVideo. Short clips at low resolution fit 24GB; high-quality long clips need 80GB.

name: Real-time TTS
description: Real-time text-to-speech with models like XTTS, StyleTTS2, or Kokoro. Requires low latency; 4–8GB VRAM is sufficient; GPU choice matters for P99 latency.

name: Vector DB queries
description: GPU-accelerated approximate nearest-neighbour search with FAISS or cuVS. VRAM scales with index size; small indexes fit on entry-level GPUs.

name: Jupyter development notebook
description: Interactive GPU-enabled Jupyter notebooks for ML experimentation, data exploration, and prototyping. Billed for the full session duration; stop when idle.

name: Batch inference
description: Process large offline datasets with maximum throughput—dataset scoring, classification, or data augmentation pipelines. Higher batch sizes saturate GPU compute more efficiently.

name: RLHF training
description: Reinforcement learning from human feedback (PPO/DPO) for aligning language models. Requires holding multiple model copies in VRAM simultaneously; multi-GPU setups are typical.

name: RAG pipeline
description: Retrieval-augmented generation combining embedding search with LLM inference. VRAM requirement is dominated by the LLM; embedding model adds 1–2GB on top.

name: Object detection
description: Real-time or batch object detection with YOLO, RT-DETR, or similar models. Modest VRAM requirements; throughput scales with GPU compute for video pipelines.

name: Image classification
description: Classify images with ViT, EfficientNet, ConvNeXt, or custom CNNs. Very low VRAM; well-suited for small, cost-effective GPUs in high-throughput batch jobs.

name: Semantic segmentation
description: Pixel-level scene understanding with SAM, SegFormer, or Mask2Former. VRAM scales with input resolution; 8GB covers most 1080p inference tasks.

name: Pose estimation
description: Estimate human or animal body poses from images or video streams using ViTPose, OpenPose, or RTMPose. Lightweight inference suitable for modest GPU instances.

name: OCR
description: Extract text from images and PDFs with TrOCR, PaddleOCR, Surya, or Tesseract with GPU acceleration. Very low VRAM; throughput improves with batching.

name: Depth estimation
description: Predict per-pixel depth from monocular or stereo images using Depth Anything v2 or ZoeDepth. Inference is fast and memory-efficient on mid-range GPUs.

name: Code generation (LLM)
description: Serve code-focused LLMs such as Qwen2.5-Coder, DeepSeek-Coder, or CodeLlama for copilot-style completions and multi-file generation. Long context windows increase VRAM needs.

name: Multimodal inference
description: Run vision-language models such as LLaVA, Qwen-VL, or Idefics for image+text understanding. VRAM depends on language model backbone size; 7B VLMs need ~18–24GB.

name: Protein folding
description: Predict protein 3D structure with AlphaFold2, ESMFold, or Boltz. VRAM scales with sequence length; long proteins require 80GB GPUs.

name: Molecular dynamics
description: GPU-accelerated molecular dynamics simulations with GROMACS, NAMD, or OpenMM. Simulation throughput scales with GPU memory bandwidth and compute.

name: Drug discovery
description: Virtual screening, generative molecular design, and ADMET prediction with graph neural networks or diffusion models such as DiffSBDD or RFDiffusion.

name: Climate modeling
description: ML-accelerated climate and weather prediction with Pangu-Weather, GraphCast, or NeuralGCM. Requires high-bandwidth multi-GPU interconnects for global-scale models.

name: 3D rendering
description: GPU-accelerated 3D rendering with Blender Cycles, Octane, or similar renderers. VRAM stores scene geometry and textures; complex scenes need 24GB+.

name: Neural radiance fields (NeRF)
description: Reconstruct 3D scenes from 2D photos using instant-ngp, Nerfacto, or 3D Gaussian Splatting. Training is GPU-intensive; a single RTX 4090 is the sweet spot.

name: GAN training
description: Train generative adversarial networks such as StyleGAN3 or BigGAN. Balanced generator/discriminator memory; benefits from large batch sizes for training stability.

name: Diffusion model training
description: Train or fine-tune diffusion models such as SDXL, DiT, or Flux from scratch. Memory-intensive due to UNet/DiT size, VAE, and text encoder; multi-GPU recommended.

name: Reinforcement learning
description: Train RL agents with PPO, SAC, or DreamerV3 in simulated or real environments. VRAM requirement depends on policy network and replay buffer size.

name: Autonomous driving simulation
description: Train and evaluate autonomous driving perception, prediction, and planning models in CARLA, Waymax, or nuPlan. Multi-sensor fusion and 3D scene understanding are VRAM-heavy.

name: Speech recognition training
description: Train or fine-tune ASR models such as Whisper, Conformer, or wav2vec 2.0 on domain-specific speech data. Long audio sequences increase memory requirements.

name: Music generation
description: Generate music and audio with MusicGen, Stable Audio, or AudioCraft. Moderate VRAM; generation time scales with output duration.
